task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the Jitsi Meet instance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows 'http://localhost:8080' or the Jitsi Meet homepage is visible.

task_completion.description: The agent joined the Jitsi Meet room named 'WebinarPrep'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows 'http://localhost:8080/WebinarPrep' or the room name 'WebinarPrep' is clearly visible in the Jitsi Meet header.

task_completion.description: The camera is active in the Jitsi Meet interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The camera icon in the bottom toolbar does not have a slash through it, indicating the camera is turned on.

task_completion.description: The user's own video feed (Self View) is hidden from the interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user's own video thumbnail/tile is not visible on the screen, or the three-dot menu shows the option 'Show self view' (indicating it is currently hidden).

task_completion.description: The camera remains active even when the self-view is hidden.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the final state where the self-view is hidden, the camera icon in the bottom toolbar still does not have a slash through it.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with Jitsi Meet.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window is open and displaying the Jitsi Meet interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to modify local storage directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are shown executing commands to modify browser local storage or making API calls to Jitsi.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Jitsi Meet application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Jitsi Meet interface is genuine and matches the expected layout, not a mock-up or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Jitsi Meet instance is hosted at http://localhost:8080. The room name is 'WebinarPrep'. The setting to hide self-view is stored as 'disableSelfView' in local storage. Visually, when the self-view is successfully hidden, the user's own video tile disappears from the screen, but the camera must remain active (the camera icon in the toolbar should not have a slash).
